meta.audience: Households with solar generation, an electric car and flexible loads

today.headline: Use the solar peak at 13:00; charge the car after 23:00
today.reason: The midday solar surplus covers one flexible appliance. The lowest synthetic grid price arrives late tonight.

actions.title: Run one flexible appliance
actions.detail: A 4.7 kW synthetic solar surplus is available.

decisionTrace.label: Signal
decisionTrace.detail: 5.4 kW forecast generation − 0.72 kW household load = 4.68 kW surplus; the price floor is €0.16/kWh at 23:00.

decisionTrace.label: Rule
decisionTrace.detail: Use local surplus for a flexible load; defer car charging only while the lower price and departure window remain feasible.

decisionTrace.label: Recommendation
decisionTrace.detail: Run one appliance at 13:00 and start the car after 23:00. WARM sends no command.

today.wouldChange: A lower solar forecast, an earlier car departure or a comfort override would change this recommendation.

decisions.title: Move car charging to low-price hours
decisions.why: The synthetic load profile contains 2,745 kWh of annual car charging and recurring price spreads.
decisions.firstStep: Create a 23:00–06:00 charging window; keep manual override available.
decisions.wouldChange: The car must leave before the low-price window, or recurring price spreads narrow.

decisions.title: Shift flexible loads into solar surplus
decisions.why: 3,440 kWh of synthetic solar generation is exported while household demand occurs later.
decisions.firstStep: Schedule one dishwasher or laundry cycle around the daily solar peak.
decisions.wouldChange: Exported generation falls, or household routines cannot move a flexible load.

decisions.title: Tune storage for evening peaks
decisions.why: The scenario shows repeatable evening imports after daytime generation.
decisions.firstStep: Compare the current 10 kWh assumption with 5 and 15 kWh in Explore.
decisions.wouldChange: Equipment cost, degradation or usable capacity outweighs the modeled energy value.
